---
title: "Day 3 — Integration & Refinement"
subtitle: "Wednesday 17 June 2026"
---

## Theme

*Revealing the realistic potential and boundaries of cross-disciplinary approaches*

Day 3 addresses the practical challenges that emerged on Day 2: censoring mechanisms, time-varying effects, computational efficiency, and identifiability. Groups refine their analysis strategies and prepare final presentations.

---

## Schedule

| Time | Activity |
|------|----------|
| 9:00–10:00 | Open discussion: ideas, challenges, and outstanding questions across all groups |
| 10:00–10:30 | Morning break |
| 10:30–12:00 | Refine case study analysis plan · Implement refined analysis · Prepare code examples |
| 12:00–14:00 | Lunch |
| 14:00–15:30 | Continue working on case studies in small groups · Prepare presentation of final analysis plan |
| 15:30–16:00 | Afternoon break |
| 16:00–17:00 | **Plenary:** Group progress reports · Identify key learnings |

---

## Learning Objectives

By the end of Day 3, participants will be able to:

1. Articulate the key challenges in applying their chosen methods to the case study data
2. Explain how their analysis plan was refined in response to Day 2 cross-group feedback
3. Describe at least one practical limitation of applying SA or IDM methods in the other discipline's context
4. Produce reproducible, annotated code implementing their analysis plan

---

## Key Challenges to Address

### Data Challenges

| Challenge | SA perspective | IDM perspective |
|-----------|---------------|-----------------|
| Censoring | Right-censoring, interval-censoring | Partially observed outbreak data |
| Reporting delays | Lagged event times | Nowcasting, back-calculation |
| Underreporting | Missing events | Ascertainment fraction estimation |
| Time-varying effects | Time-varying covariates, landmarking | Non-stationary transmission rates |

### Computational Challenges

- Model fitting in high-dimensional parameter spaces
- Bayesian vs. frequentist approaches
- Identifiability and estimability from aggregate data

---

## Materials

### Refined Analysis Plans

*Groups will post their refined analysis plan documents here*

### Code

*Annotated code examples will be shared here after the workshop*

### Key Learnings Summary

*A summary of key learnings identified in the Day 3 plenary will be posted here*
